choices: too extreme or excessive
slightly more than expected
japanese: やり過ぎ、度が過ぎる
explanation: Used when something is unreasonable or more than you can accept.

choices: very easy to do
a pleasant reward for effort
japanese: 朝飯前、とても簡単なこと
explanation: Describes a task that requires little effort or skill.

choices: make a bad situation worse
bring energy to a struggling effort
japanese: 火に油を注ぐ、状況を悪化させる
explanation: Means to worsen an already tense or difficult situation.

choices: ready and eager to listen
paying close attention to many things at once
japanese: じっくり聞く準備ができている
explanation: Said when you are fully attentive and ready to hear what someone has to say.

choices: happening unexpectedly and quickly
happening after a long wait
japanese: 突然、急に
explanation: Means something happened without warning or preparation.

choices: no matter what it takes
while keeping expenses low
literal: どんな代価を払っても
japanese: 何がなんでも、どんな犠牲を払っても
explanation: Means you will do something regardless of the effort or sacrifice required.

choices: having to start over from the beginning
returning to a simpler approach
japanese: 振り出しに戻る、最初からやり直す
explanation: Means all progress has been lost and you must begin again.

choices: pursue the wrong person or solution
make noise without achieving results
japanese: 見当違いのことをする、的外れな追及をする
explanation: Means you are directing your efforts or blame at the wrong target.

choices: avoid getting to the main point
take a slow and careful approach to a problem
japanese: 遠回しに言う、要点を避ける
explanation: Describes talking around a topic without directly addressing it.

choices: try extremely hard to help or please someone
make an uncomfortable compromise
japanese: 人のために懸命に努力する、無理をして尽くす
explanation: Means to make a great effort to accommodate or assist someone.

choices: endure something painful or difficult
make a quick and risky decision
japanese: 歯を食いしばって耐える、覚悟を決める
explanation: Means to accept and endure an unpleasant or difficult situation.

choices: reduce tension and start a conversation
be the first to raise a difficult topic
japanese: 場の緊張をほぐす、会話のきっかけを作る
explanation: Means to do or say something to make people feel more comfortable in a social situation.

choices: work very late into the night
spend too much energy on one task
japanese: 夜遅くまで働く・勉強する
explanation: Describes staying up very late to work or study.

choices: stop working for the day
declare a project finished
japanese: 今日はここまでにする、仕事を切り上げる
explanation: Means to decide to stop an activity for the rest of the day.

choices: surprise someone when they are unprepared
notice someone making a mistake
literal: 誰かの警戒を捕まえる
japanese: 不意をつく、油断しているところを突く
explanation: Means to surprise someone at a moment when they are not ready or expecting it.

choices: be useful at the right moment
arrive just in time to help
japanese: 役に立つ、都合よく使える
explanation: Means something turns out to be useful when needed.

idiom: Cost an Arm and a Leg
choices: be extremely expensive
require a lot of time and energy
japanese: 非常に高価である、法外な値段がする
explanation: Describes something that costs a very large amount of money.

choices: do something poorly to save time or money
find a more efficient way to complete a task
japanese: 手を抜く、近道をして質を落とす
explanation: Means to do a job with less care or effort than is required.

choices: get to the important point without delay
make a quick decision under pressure
japanese: 要点に入る、前置きを省いて本題に入る
explanation: Means to skip unnecessary details and focus on what really matters.

choices: do something helpful for someone
return something owed to someone
japanese: 人の頼みを聞く、親切にしてあげる
explanation: Means to do a helpful or kind act for another person.

choices: please don't misunderstand what I'm saying
don't let me make a mistake
literal: 私を間違えて取らないで
japanese: 誤解しないでほしい、そういう意味じゃない
explanation: Said to clarify your intention and prevent a misunderstanding.

choices: practical, realistic, and unpretentious
calm and relaxed under pressure
literal: 地球に向かって下りる
japanese: 現実的でざっくばらんな、気取らない
explanation: Describes a person who is sensible, humble, and not affected.

choices: do something slowly and carefully
stop worrying and just try it
japanese: 落ち着いてゆっくりやれ、慌てずに
explanation: Used to tell someone to proceed gently or without rushing.

choices: accept the unpleasant consequences of your actions
be honest about a difficult situation
japanese: 自分の行動の結果を受け入れる、観念する
explanation: Means to accept punishment or criticism for something you have done.

choices: break down or stop working completely
slowly lose interest in something
japanese: 崩壊する、ばらばらになる
explanation: Means something or someone is breaking down and no longer functioning.

choices: feel slightly sick or unwell
feel overwhelmed by stress or pressure
japanese: 体の具合が悪い、気分がすぐれない
explanation: Describes feeling ill or not in good physical condition.

choices: find a solution or understand something
make a plan for handling something
japanese: 解決策を見つける、理解する
explanation: Means to discover an answer or work out how to deal with something.

choices: discover shared interests or agreements
avoid disagreement by changing the topic
literal: 共通の土台を見つける
japanese: 共通点を見つける、意見が一致する点を探す
explanation: Means to identify areas of agreement between people with differing views.

choices: become nervous and hesitate to do something
lose interest in a plan you once liked
japanese: 尻込みする、怖気づく
explanation: Means to suddenly feel afraid or uncertain about doing something you planned.

choices: become impossible to control
go further than originally planned
japanese: 手に負えなくなる、収拾がつかなくなる
explanation: Means a situation has become uncontrollable or unmanageable.

choices: learn how to do something through practice
finally understand the reason for something
japanese: コツをつかむ、慣れてうまくできるようになる
explanation: Means to gradually become skilled or comfortable with something.

choices: help someone with a task
applaud someone for their achievement
japanese: 手を貸す、手伝う
explanation: Means to assist someone with what they are doing.

choices: make more effort than is required
continue doing something longer than necessary without purpose
japanese: 一歩踏み込んで努力する、期待以上のことをする
explanation: Means to put in more effort than what is normally expected.

choices: keep trying despite difficulties
stay calm and wait for things to improve
literal: そこにぶら下がっていろ
japanese: 頑張り続ける、諦めないで
explanation: Encourages someone to persist through a tough situation.

idiom: Hit the Nail on the Head
choices: say or do exactly the right thing
solve a problem with a direct approach
japanese: ずばり言い当てる、核心をつく
explanation: Means to describe something perfectly or identify the exact cause of a problem.

choices: leave and start a journey
begin working on something immediately
japanese: 出発する、旅に出る
explanation: Means to depart or begin traveling somewhere.

choices: go to bed and sleep
stop working and take a rest
japanese: 寝る、就寝する
explanation: An informal way of saying it's time to go to sleep.

choices: in serious trouble or difficulty
under pressure to perform well
japanese: 窮地に立っている、問題を抱えている
explanation: Describes being in a situation that has caused trouble or got you into trouble.

choices: considering what will happen over time
after putting in sustained effort
japanese: 長い目で見れば、最終的には
explanation: Refers to what will be true or beneficial over a long period of time.

choices: join a trend or activity because it is popular
take advantage of an opportunity quickly
literal: バンドワゴンに飛び乗る
japanese: 流行に乗る、人気に便乗する
explanation: Means to follow a trend or join something simply because everyone else is doing it.

choices: watch or monitor someone or something carefully
check something only occasionally
japanese: 目を離さない、見張る
explanation: Means to pay close attention to or supervise something or someone.

choices: manage to survive a difficult situation
stay focused during a stressful time
japanese: なんとか持ちこたえる、溺れずに済む
explanation: Means to barely manage to cope with problems or financial difficulties.

idiom: Kill Two Birds with One Stone
choices: accomplish two things with a single action
solve two problems by dealing with the most important one
literal: 一石で二羽の鳥を殺す
japanese: 一石二鳥、一つの行動で二つの目的を達成する
explanation: Means to achieve two goals at the same time with one effort.

idiom: Let the Cat Out of the Bag
choices: accidentally reveal a secret
bring a hidden problem into the open
japanese: うっかり秘密をしゃべる、秘密が漏れる
explanation: Means to reveal information that was supposed to be kept secret.

choices: focus on the positive aspects of a situation
remain hopeful that things will improve
japanese: 物事の良い面を見る、前向きに考える
explanation: Encourages someone to think about the good things in a bad situation.

choices: have just enough money to pay for basic needs
manage to finish a project with limited resources
japanese: なんとか生活費を賄う、収支を合わせる
explanation: Means to have barely enough money to cover all your expenses.

choices: lose an opportunity by acting too late
fail to understand what is happening
japanese: チャンスを逃す、機を逸する
explanation: Means to miss a chance to do something because you were too slow.

choices: you must work hard to achieve results
if something doesn't hurt, it probably won't work
literal: 痛みがなければ得るものもない
japanese: 苦労なくして成果なし、努力なしには成功しない
explanation: Means that achieving goals requires effort and hardship.

choices: having the same understanding or agreement
working toward the same goal at the same time
japanese: 同じ認識を持っている、意思が一致している
explanation: Means everyone involved has the same information and understanding.

choices: very rarely
at an unexpected and surprising moment
japanese: めったにない、ごくたまに
explanation: Describes something that happens very infrequently.

choices: suddenly and unexpectedly
in a way that nobody could prevent
japanese: 突然、何の前触れもなく
explanation: Means something happened with no warning at all.

choices: exaggerated or too extreme
successfully completing something difficult
japanese: やり過ぎ、大げさすぎる
explanation: Describes behavior or something that is unnecessarily excessive or exaggerated.

choices: joke with or tease someone
pressure someone into doing something
japanese: 冗談を言う、からかう
explanation: Means to tell someone something untrue as a joke.

idiom: Put All Your Eggs in One Basket
choices: rely on only one plan or option
invest all your energy in a single goal
literal: 一つのかごに全ての卵を入れる
japanese: 一つのことだけに賭ける、リスクを分散しない
explanation: Warns against depending entirely on one option with no backup plan.

choices: a general guideline based on experience
the most reliable way to do something
japanese: 経験則、おおよその基準
explanation: Refers to a practical principle based on experience rather than exact calculation.

choices: lose energy or motivation to continue
fail to complete something due to lack of time
japanese: 勢いが尽きる、やる気がなくなる
explanation: Means to become too tired or unmotivated to keep going.

choices: agree with someone on a topic
have an honest conversation with someone
japanese: 意見が一致する、同じ考えを持つ
explanation: Means to have the same opinion or view as another person.

choices: wait patiently without doing anything
stay calm and avoid panicking
japanese: じっと待つ、辛抱する
explanation: Means to stay where you are and wait calmly for something to happen.

choices: said when someone appears just as you were talking about them
said when a bad situation gets even worse
japanese: うわさをすれば影、ちょうど話題にしていた人が現れる
explanation: An expression used when the person you were just discussing suddenly arrives.

choices: reveal secret information
tell the whole truth about something
japanese: 秘密をしゃべる、うっかり打ち明ける
explanation: Means to accidentally or carelessly reveal information that was secret.

choices: relax and not work too hard
not worry too much about something
japanese: ゆっくりする、のんびりする
explanation: Means to relax, rest, or avoid putting too much pressure on yourself.

choices: fail to appreciate something you have
assume something is true without checking
literal: 当然のものとして受け取る
japanese: 当たり前と思う、ありがたみを忘れる
explanation: Means to not appreciate something because you assume it will always be there.

idiom: Take the Bull by the Horns
choices: deal with a difficult problem directly and confidently
face a dangerous situation without hesitation
japanese: 困難に果敢に立ち向かう、積極的に問題に取り組む
explanation: Means to confront a challenging situation in a bold and direct way.

idiom: The Ball Is in Your Court
choices: it is your turn to take action or make a decision
you have the advantage in a situation
literal: ボールはあなたのコートにある
japanese: 次はあなたが行動する番だ、あとはあなた次第
explanation: Means the responsibility for the next action or decision belongs to you.

choices: the final problem that makes a situation unbearable
the smallest issue in a series of bigger problems
japanese: 堪忍袋の緒が切れる原因、もう限界だと感じさせる最後の一撃
explanation: Refers to the final small problem that causes someone to completely lose patience.

choices: approach problems in a creative and unconventional way
consider all possible options before deciding
japanese: 固定観念にとらわれずに考える、斬新なアイデアを出す
explanation: Encourages creative thinking that goes beyond traditional or expected solutions.

choices: give up and admit defeat
stop trying to avoid a confrontation
japanese: 諦める、降参する
explanation: Means to admit that you have been beaten and stop trying.

choices: time passes very quickly
there is never enough time to do everything
japanese: 時間があっという間に過ぎる
explanation: Expresses that time seems to pass very quickly, especially during enjoyable activities.

choices: deliberately ignore something wrong
avoid getting involved in someone else's problem
japanese: 見て見ぬふりをする、故意に無視する
explanation: Means to choose to ignore something you know is wrong or harmful.

choices: feeling sick or unwell
feeling sad or emotionally low
japanese: 体の具合が悪い、気分がすぐれない
explanation: Describes feeling physically ill or not in the best health.

choices: uncertain and not yet decided
optimistic and full of possibilities
japanese: 未定で宙ぶらりんな状態、まだ決まっていない
explanation: Means a situation is still undecided or unresolved.

choices: be very careful to avoid upsetting someone
move cautiously in an unfamiliar situation
japanese: 腫れ物に触るように接する、慎重に言葉を選ぶ
explanation: Means to be extremely cautious about what you say or do to avoid angering someone.

choices: spend time on useless or unproductive activities
miss an opportunity due to slow action
japanese: 時間を無駄遣いする、無意味なことに時間を費やす
explanation: Means to use time on things that have no value or result.

choices: information spread by people talking to each other
a recommendation given directly to one person
japanese: 口コミ、人から人への情報伝達
explanation: Refers to information or recommendations passed along through personal conversations.

choices: work very hard for a long time
do unpleasant tasks without complaining
japanese: 猛烈に働く、馬車馬のように働く
explanation: Means to put in an enormous amount of hard work.

idiom: You Can Lead a Horse to Water
choices: you can give someone an opportunity but you can't force them to use it
you can guide someone but they may not follow your advice
literal: 馬を水辺に連れて行ける
japanese: 機会を与えることはできても、本人にやる気がなければどうにもならない
explanation: Means you can provide the opportunity but cannot force someone to take advantage of it.

idiom: Your Guess Is as Good as Mine
choices: I have no idea, just like you
we both have an equal chance of being right
literal: あなたの推測は私のと同じくらい良い
japanese: 私にもわからない、あなたと同様見当もつかない
explanation: Means the speaker doesn't know the answer any better than the person asking.

choices: focus directly on a specific target or issue
gradually reduce options until one remains
japanese: 焦点を絞る、的を絞る
explanation: Means to direct all your attention toward one specific thing.

choices: something that seems bad but turns out good
something that seems good but turns out bad
japanese: 不幸に見えて実は幸運、禍転じて福となす
explanation: Refers to something that initially seemed unfortunate but resulted in something positive.

choices: a situation where something bad almost happened
a difficult decision with two equally good options
japanese: 危機一髪、間一髪の出来事
explanation: Describes a situation where disaster was narrowly avoided.

choices: something unlikely to succeed
a plan that will take a very long time
japanese: 可能性が低いこと、見込みの薄い試み
explanation: Means an attempt that has a very low chance of success.

choices: something that costs far more than it is worth
a product or service that fails to meet expectations
japanese: ぼったくり、不当に高い値段
explanation: Describes a purchase or deal where you paid far too much for what you received.

choices: a difficult problem or a hard person to deal with
a task that requires more time than expected
literal: 割れにくいかたい木の実
japanese: 難しい問題・手ごわい相手、一筋縄ではいかない
explanation: Refers to something or someone that is difficult to solve or influence.

choices: in a better position than expected
being well prepared compared to others
japanese: 有利な状況にある、先を行っている
explanation: Means to be well-prepared or more advanced than what is required.

choices: returning to the right path after a problem
recovering after a personal setback
japanese: 軌道修正する、元の状態に戻る
explanation: Means to return to normal progress after a disruption.

choices: out of public view, working in the background
in a private way that avoids attention
japanese: 舞台裏で、表に出ずに
explanation: Refers to activity happening in the background, away from public view.

choices: release stress or anger through an activity
express strong feelings openly to someone
japanese: うっぷんを晴らす、ストレスを発散する
explanation: Means to release frustration or tension through an activity.

choices: have neither a profit nor a loss
recover the money you originally spent
japanese: 収支がトントンになる、損益ゼロになる
explanation: Means to reach a point where income equals expenses, resulting in no gain or loss.

choices: reveal something that was hidden or unknown
explain something complicated in a clear way
japanese: 明るみに出す、暴露する
explanation: Means to expose or make something previously unknown available to others.

choices: certainly, please go ahead
using every possible method available
japanese: もちろん、どうぞ遠慮なく
explanation: Expresses enthusiastic permission or agreement.

choices: be in charge and make the decisions
help others make important decisions
japanese: 仕切る、決定権を持つ
explanation: Means to be the person who has authority to make important decisions.

choices: tell the truth after hiding something
apologize sincerely for a mistake
japanese: 白状する、正直に打ち明ける
explanation: Means to finally confess or tell the truth about something you had kept secret.

choices: go beyond what is acceptable or appropriate
break a rule that was clearly stated
japanese: 一線を越える、許容範囲を超えた行動をする
explanation: Means to behave in a way that exceeds the limits of what is acceptable.

choices: be upset about something that cannot be changed
make a big deal out of a small mistake
literal: こぼれたミルクのために泣く
japanese: 取り返しのつかないことを嘆く、後悔しても仕方がない
explanation: Means to feel sad or upset about something that has already happened and cannot be fixed.

choices: make a mistake or fail to complete a responsibility
give up on something at a crucial moment
japanese: 失敗する、責任を果たせない
explanation: Means to fail at a task or let someone down by not doing what was expected.

choices: something is much harder to do than it sounds
a task takes more time than people expect
literal: 言うは易く行うは難し
japanese: 言うは易し行うは難し
explanation: Used to say that something sounds simple but is actually quite difficult in practice.

idiom: Every Cloud Has a Silver Lining
choices: every bad situation has some positive aspect
things always improve if you wait long enough
literal: すべての雲には銀の縁がある
japanese: どんなことにも良い面がある、苦あれば楽あり
explanation: Encourages optimism by noting that even difficult situations have something good in them.

choices: complete what you started or promised
continue doing something even when it gets difficult
japanese: やり遂げる、最後まで実行する
explanation: Means to complete an action or fulfill a commitment to the end.

choices: return to the correct path after a setback
regain focus after losing concentration
japanese: 軌道修正して元に戻る、やり直す
explanation: Means to resume normal progress after experiencing a problem or delay.

choices: try something even if success is uncertain
make your best effort on something challenging
literal: それに一発やってみる
japanese: 試しにやってみる、とにかく挑戦する
explanation: Encourages someone to attempt something without worrying too much about the outcome.

idiom: Give Someone the Benefit of the Doubt
choices: trust someone despite having some uncertainty
forgive someone for a past mistake
literal: 誰かに疑いの利益を与える
japanese: 疑わしくてもひとまず信用する、善意に解釈する
explanation: Means to choose to believe someone's good intentions even without full proof.

choices: act in a way that goes against what most people do or believe
do something difficult that requires extra effort
japanese: 流れに逆らう、一般的な考えに反する
explanation: Means to do or say something that is contrary to what is generally accepted or expected.

choices: accept what is happening and adapt naturally
ignore plans and act without thinking
japanese: 流れに身を任せる、自然の成り行きに従う
explanation: Means to accept a situation and adapt to it rather than fighting against it.

choices: be in a very fragile or dangerous situation
depend entirely on one person or thing for survival
literal: 一本の糸でぶら下がる
japanese: 一触即発の状態にある、今にも崩れそうな状況
explanation: Means something is in a very precarious state and could fail at any moment.

choices: change your opinion or decision about something
become more sympathetic after thinking carefully
japanese: 気が変わる、考えが変わる
explanation: Means to reverse your previous decision or change how you feel about something.

choices: have more important things to deal with
be too busy to pay attention right now
literal: もっと大きな魚を揚げる
japanese: もっと重要なことがある、もっと大事な問題を抱えている
explanation: Means you have more significant matters to attend to than the current topic.

choices: achieve great success or win something valuable
get extremely lucky in an unexpected situation
japanese: 大当たりする、大成功を収める
explanation: Means to experience a great success or receive an unexpectedly large reward.

choices: slow down and wait before acting
stay calm and don't get excited
japanese: ちょっと待て、焦るな
explanation: Means to pause and slow down before doing something hasty.

choices: in the same difficult situation as others
facing the same challenge with the same resources
japanese: 同じ立場にある、同じ困難を共有している
explanation: Means sharing the same problems or circumstances as others.

choices: both people are responsible for a situation
cooperation is needed to achieve something
literal: タンゴを踊るには二人必要
japanese: どちらにも責任がある、一方だけが悪いわけではない
explanation: Means that both people involved in a conflict or situation share the responsibility.

choices: act too soon before the right time
react too strongly to something minor
japanese: 早まった行動をする、フライングする
explanation: Means to start something or draw a conclusion before the proper time.

choices: prevent something from getting closer or worsening
manage something carefully so it doesn't become a problem
literal: 何かを湾に留めておく
japanese: 近づけないようにする、抑えておく
explanation: Means to control or restrain something so it cannot cause harm.

choices: stay calm when in a stressful or frustrating situation
avoid saying something you will regret
japanese: 冷静を保つ、感情的にならない
explanation: Means to remain calm and controlled in a difficult or provoking situation.

choices: be familiar with how something works
understand all the rules of a system
japanese: 要領を知っている、仕事のコツを知っている
explanation: Means to understand the details and procedures of a job or activity.

choices: try every possible option to achieve something
be very thorough and careful in your work
literal: どの石も裏返さずにおかない
japanese: あらゆる手を尽くす、石にかじりついても
explanation: Means to make every possible effort in order to find or achieve something.

choices: avoid bringing up problems that have been forgotten
don't challenge someone who is already calm
literal: 眠っている犬をそのままにしておく
japanese: 寝た子を起こすな、問題を蒸し返すな
explanation: Means to leave something alone that could cause trouble if brought up again.

choices: give only the essential details to summarize something
skip over the unimportant parts of a story
japanese: 手短に言うと、かいつまんで言えば
explanation: Said when you want to summarize a complex story or situation briefly.

choices: behave in an embarrassing or dramatic way in public
draw unwanted attention to a situation
japanese: 大騒ぎをする、人前で恥ずかしい振る舞いをする
explanation: Means to cause a public disturbance through emotional or dramatic behavior.

choices: feel free to relax and be comfortable
act as though you belong somewhere
literal: 家にいるようにしなさい
japanese: くつろいでください、遠慮しないで
explanation: An invitation to feel relaxed and comfortable in someone else's space.

choices: reach a compromise by both sides giving something up
make the first move in resolving a conflict
japanese: 妥協点を見つける、お互いに歩み寄る
explanation: Means to reach an agreement by both parties making concessions.

choices: with no conditions or hidden obligations
without expecting anything in return
japanese: 条件なし、見返りを求めない
explanation: Means something is given or offered freely with no hidden requirements or commitments.

idiom: Not Playing with a Full Deck
choices: not very intelligent or mentally sharp
unable to think clearly due to stress
literal: フルデッキでプレイしていない
japanese: 頭の回転が鈍い、少し変わっている
explanation: An informal way to say someone is not very smart or is acting foolishly.

choices: freed from a responsibility or difficult situation
no longer blamed for something that went wrong
japanese: お咎めなし、責任を免れる
explanation: Means to be released from an obligation or to escape punishment.

choices: extremely happy and excited
in a daydream, not paying attention to reality
japanese: 有頂天になっている、最高に幸せな状態
explanation: Describes a feeling of extreme happiness or joy.

choices: alert, focused, and doing things well
working hard and making steady progress
japanese: てきぱきしている、機敏で有能な
explanation: Means to be alert, efficient, and competent in what you are doing.

choices: undecided between two options
staying neutral in a conflict between others
japanese: どちらとも決めかねている、態度を保留している
explanation: Describes someone who has not yet made a decision or taken a side.

choices: in a risky situation where a mistake could cause trouble
doing something that most people would disagree with
japanese: 危険な状況にある、一歩間違えると大変なことになる
explanation: Means to be in a dangerous or delicate situation where any wrong move could cause problems.

choices: beyond control and impossible to manage
happening faster than anyone anticipated
japanese: 手に負えない、収拾がつかない
explanation: Describes a situation that has become uncontrollable.

choices: behaving in an unacceptable or rude way
acting differently from what everyone else does
japanese: 礼儀に反する、常識外れの行動をとる
explanation: Means to behave in a way that is considered improper or disrespectful.

choices: no longer in danger or difficulty
past the most complicated part of a task
japanese: 危機を脱した、難局を乗り越えた
explanation: Means to have escaped a dangerous or difficult situation.

choices: decide what to do as the situation develops
stay flexible and avoid committing to a plan
japanese: 臨機応変に対応する、その場の状況で決める
explanation: Means to handle a situation based on how it develops rather than following a set plan.

choices: argue the opposite side to stimulate discussion
pretend to agree while secretly disagreeing
literal: 悪魔の代弁者を演じる
japanese: あえて反対意見を言う、議論を活発にするために逆の立場を取る
explanation: Means to present an opposing view for the sake of argument or to test an idea.

choices: stop or end something completely
make a final and irreversible decision
japanese: 終止符を打つ、プロジェクトなどを打ち切る
explanation: Means to stop something from continuing, often an activity or project.

choices: firmly refuse to accept something or insist on a decision
take a strong first step to start something
japanese: 断固とした態度を取る、はっきり意思を伝える
explanation: Means to firmly assert your authority or make it clear you will not tolerate something.

choices: understand a hidden meaning beyond what is said
pay close attention to the exact words someone uses
japanese: 行間を読む、言外の意味をくみ取る
explanation: Means to look for a meaning that is implied but not directly stated.

choices: sound familiar or remind you of something
draw someone's attention to something important
japanese: 聞き覚えがある、思い当たる
explanation: Means that something sounds familiar or triggers a memory.

choices: disturb a stable situation by causing trouble
take risks that put a shared effort in danger
japanese: 波風を立てる、物事を混乱させる
explanation: Means to cause problems or create conflict in a situation that was previously stable.

choices: irritate or annoy someone unintentionally
say something that offends someone directly
japanese: 人の神経を逆撫でする、気に障ることをする
explanation: Means to make someone feel annoyed or uncomfortable, often without intending to.

choices: keep something in reserve for a future time of need
avoid spending money on things that aren't necessary
literal: 雨の日のために取っておく
japanese: まさかの時のために蓄えておく、将来に備える
explanation: Means to set aside resources for when they might be needed in the future.

choices: understand the whole situation rather than focusing on details
realize how serious a situation is
japanese: 全体像を見る、大局を把握する
explanation: Means to consider the overall situation rather than concentrating on small details.

choices: correct a misunderstanding or false information
give your version of events to make things fair
japanese: 誤解を正す、事実をはっきりさせる
explanation: Means to give the correct information to replace something that was wrong or misunderstood.

choices: wait until tomorrow before making a decision
think carefully about something before responding
japanese: 一晩考えてから決める、即断せずに考える
explanation: Means to delay a decision until the next day after having more time to think.

choices: say honestly what you think or feel
stand up for your opinions even when challenged
japanese: 思っていることを率直に言う、本音を言う
explanation: Means to express your true thoughts or feelings openly and directly.

choices: refuse to change your position despite pressure
defend yourself when someone challenges you
japanese: 自分の立場を守る、主張を曲げない
explanation: Means to maintain your opinion or position even when others push back.

choices: take attention or credit away from someone else
do something impressive before someone else gets the chance
japanese: 人の手柄を横取りする、注目をさらう
explanation: Means to take the attention or praise that was meant for someone else.

choices: decline now but suggest doing it another time
postpone something because of bad circumstances
japanese: 今回は断るが今度誘ってほしい、後日にする
explanation: Means to politely refuse an invitation with the intention of accepting at a later time.

idiom: Take with a Grain of Salt
choices: be somewhat skeptical about what you hear
consider something carefully before accepting it
literal: 塩ひとつまみと一緒に受け取る
japanese: 話半分に聞く、鵜呑みにしない
explanation: Means to hear something but not completely believe or trust it.

choices: enjoy the advantages of two different things at once
having to choose between two good things
japanese: 両方の良いところを得る、一石二鳥の状況
explanation: Describes a situation where you can enjoy all the benefits of two different things.

choices: an obvious problem that nobody wants to talk about
an important issue that everyone is aware of but avoids
japanese: 誰もが知っているのに口に出さない問題、触れにくい話題
explanation: Refers to a significant issue that is being deliberately ignored or avoided in conversation.

choices: only a small visible part of a much bigger problem
the beginning of a much larger challenge to come
japanese: 氷山の一角、表面に出ているのはほんの一部
explanation: Means that what is visible or known is only a small part of something much larger.

choices: everything possible, the complete amount
every step of a long and complicated process
japanese: 全部、何もかも
explanation: Means everything that is possible or the full extent of something.

choices: betray someone to avoid blame yourself
criticize someone in front of others to gain advantage
literal: 誰かをバスの下に投げる
japanese: 人を犠牲にして自分が助かる、責任を他人に押しつける
explanation: Means to place blame on someone else to protect yourself from consequences.

choices: get married
make a serious long-term commitment
japanese: 結婚する
explanation: An informal expression meaning to get married.

choices: wasting time is like wasting money
you must pay for every hour of work done
japanese: 時は金なり、時間を無駄にすることはお金を損すること
explanation: Means that time is a valuable resource and should not be wasted.

choices: in addition to the existing problems, something else bad happened
considering how serious the situation already is
literal: 物事をさらに悪くするために
japanese: さらに悪いことに、追い打ちをかけるように
explanation: Used to introduce an additional problem that makes a bad situation even worse.

choices: reverse a situation so the advantage shifts to you
change the topic when a conversation becomes uncomfortable
literal: テーブルをひっくり返す
japanese: 形勢を逆転させる、立場が逆になる
explanation: Means to reverse the situation so the person who was losing is now winning.

choices: pressure or persuade someone to do something they don't want to do
force someone to agree through threats
japanese: 無理強いする、強引に説得する
explanation: Means to strongly persuade or pressure someone into doing something reluctantly.

choices: right in front of someone, in an obvious place
happening secretly without anyone noticing
japanese: 目の前で、すぐそこで
explanation: Means happening very close to someone, often without them noticing.

choices: it is your decision to make
you are responsible for the result
japanese: あなた次第、あなたが決めていいよ
explanation: Means the choice or decision belongs to the other person.

choices: do what you say you will do
show through actions that your words are sincere
japanese: 有言実行、言ったことを実際に行動に移す
explanation: Means to act in a way that is consistent with what you claim or believe.

choices: have many different roles or responsibilities
be able to adapt to many different situations
japanese: 多くの役割を担う、様々な責任を持つ
explanation: Means to perform several different jobs or fill many different roles.

choices: survive a difficult period without giving up
get through a crisis without losing what matters
japanese: 試練を乗り越える、困難な時期を耐え抜く
explanation: Means to endure and survive a difficult or challenging time.

choices: something will never happen
something will only happen under impossible conditions
japanese: 絶対にありえない、そんなことは起こるわけがない
explanation: Used to say that something is completely impossible or will never occur.

choices: extremely valuable or useful
expensive but worth paying for
literal: その重さと同じだけの金の価値がある
japanese: 非常に価値がある、かけがえのない
explanation: Describes something or someone that is extremely valuable or beneficial.

choices: yes, definitely or of course
I'm confident that will happen
japanese: もちろん、その通り
explanation: An informal way to strongly agree or confirm something.

idiom: You Can't Have Your Cake and Eat It Too
choices: you can't enjoy two things that are incompatible
you have to choose between a reward and keeping it
literal: ケーキを持っていて食べることはできない
japanese: 二つを同時に得ることはできない、どちらかを選ばなければならない
explanation: Means you cannot have the benefits of two options that are mutually exclusive.

idiom: You Get What You Pay For
choices: quality depends on how much you spend
cheap options are not always worth choosing
literal: お金を払った分だけのものが手に入る
japanese: 安かろう悪かろう、値段相応のものが手に入る
explanation: Means that cheaper things are often of lower quality than more expensive ones.

idiom: Your Heart Is in the Right Place
choices: you have good intentions even if your actions are wrong
you care deeply about the people around you
literal: あなたの心は正しい場所にある
japanese: 気持ちは正しい、悪気はない
explanation: Means someone has good intentions even though the result may not be what was hoped.

choices: a very small amount compared to what is needed
a minor contribution that has little effect
japanese: 焼け石に水、必要量と比べてわずかな量
explanation: Describes an amount so small that it makes almost no difference.

choices: something very difficult to find
a task that requires a lot of patience
japanese: 見つけるのが非常に難しいもの、砂の中から針を探すようなもの
explanation: Describes something that is almost impossible to locate because it is hidden among many other things.

choices: a big fuss about something unimportant
a conflict that feels serious but resolves quickly
literal: ティーカップの中の嵐
japanese: コップの中の嵐、小さなことを大げさに騒ぐ
explanation: Means a great deal of excitement over something that is actually very minor.

choices: make a bad situation even worse by doing something unkind
criticize someone who is already in a difficult position
japanese: 泣き面に蜂、踏んだり蹴ったりにする
explanation: Means to worsen an already painful situation by doing or saying something hurtful.

idiom: At the End of the Day
choices: when everything is considered, ultimately
after all the work is done
japanese: 結局のところ、つまるところ
explanation: Means ultimately, when you consider everything, or in the final analysis.

choices: start planning again after a failure
reconsider a decision that turned out to be wrong
japanese: 最初から計画し直す、やり直しになる
explanation: Means to abandon a plan and begin the planning process from the beginning.

idiom: Bite Off More Than You Can Chew
choices: take on more responsibility than you can handle
make a commitment you cannot realistically keep
literal: 噛めないほど大きく噛む
japanese: 手に余ることを引き受ける、身の丈以上のことをしようとする
explanation: Means to attempt or take on more than you are capable of handling.

choices: permanently damage a relationship so there is no way back
end something in a way that causes problems later
japanese: 後に引けない状況にする、関係を完全に壊す
explanation: Means to destroy a relationship or opportunity in a way that cannot be undone.

idiom: By the Skin of Your Teeth
choices: only just manage to do something, with almost no margin
succeed at the last possible moment
japanese: 間一髪で、かろうじて
explanation: Means to barely avoid failure or danger by the smallest possible margin.

idiom: Can't Put Your Finger on It
choices: unable to identify exactly what something is
unable to remember a specific detail
literal: それに指を当てられない
japanese: はっきりわからない、何か気になるが特定できない
explanation: Means you sense something is there but cannot precisely identify or describe it.

choices: whatever happens, without fail
even in the most difficult circumstances
literal: 雨が降っても晴れていても
japanese: どんな状況でも、必ず
explanation: Means something will happen no matter what conditions or circumstances arise.

choices: raise false alarms so often that people stop believing you
warn others about a danger they don't take seriously
japanese: オオカミが来たと嘘をつく、嘘の警告を繰り返す
explanation: Means to repeatedly give false warnings until no one believes you when the real danger comes.

choices: being too nosy can lead to trouble
searching for answers can have unexpected consequences
japanese: 好奇心は危険を招く、詮索しすぎると痛い目に遭う
explanation: A warning that excessive curiosity can lead to problems or harm.

choices: be less strict or critical toward someone
give someone more time to complete something
japanese: 大目に見る、手加減する
explanation: Means to allow someone more freedom or be more forgiving of their mistakes.

idiom: Don't Count Your Chickens Before They Hatch
choices: don't assume success before it actually happens
don't spend money or resources before you have them
literal: ひよこが孵る前に数えるな
japanese: 取らぬ狸の皮算用、まだ確定していないことを当てにするな
explanation: Warns against planning based on something that has not yet been confirmed.

idiom: Don't Put All Your Eggs in One Basket
choices: don't depend on only one option or plan
spread your risks by trying several different approaches
literal: 全ての卵を一つのかごに入れるな
japanese: 一か所に全てを賭けるな、リスクを分散させよ
explanation: Advises dividing your resources or efforts to avoid losing everything if one plan fails.

choices: everyone will have a moment of success or luck
even the most unlikely person can succeed eventually
literal: どの犬にもその日がある
japanese: 誰にでも良い時がある、運はいつか向いてくる
explanation: Means everyone gets a chance to be successful or have good luck at some point.

idiom: Go Back to the Drawing Board
choices: start the planning process over from scratch
review what went wrong before trying again
japanese: ゼロから計画し直す、最初からやり直す
explanation: Means to completely restart the planning or design process after something failed.

choices: fail spectacularly and completely
be heavily criticized and lose support
japanese: 派手に失敗する、惨敗する
explanation: Means to experience a dramatic and total failure.

choices: do or say something that is unfair or cruel
attack someone when they are in a vulnerable position
japanese: 卑怯なことをする、反則技を使う
explanation: Means to use an unfair or cruel action that goes against the rules of fair behavior.

idiom: If It Ain't Broke, Don't Fix It
choices: don't change something that is already working well
avoid making unnecessary improvements
literal: 壊れていないなら直すな
japanese: 問題がないなら手を加えるな、うまくいっているものは変えるな
explanation: Advises against changing something that is functioning well simply for the sake of change.

idiom: In the Blink of an Eye
choices: extremely quickly, almost instantly
so fast it was hard to notice it happened
literal: 目をパチクリする間に
japanese: あっという間に、瞬く間に
explanation: Means something happened in an extremely short amount of time.

idiom: In the Heat of the Moment
choices: while feeling very emotional and not thinking clearly
during the most intense part of a tense situation
japanese: 感情的になっている瞬間に、興奮のあまり
explanation: Describes doing or saying something without thinking, driven by strong emotion.

choices: do many difficult or unnecessary things to achieve something
follow a complicated set of rules to complete a process
japanese: 面倒な手順を踏む、無理難題をこなす
explanation: Means to go through a lot of trouble or follow many complicated steps to get something done.

choices: stay positive and hopeful during a difficult time
don't let criticism or failure bring you down
japanese: くじけるな、元気を出して
explanation: Encouragement to remain cheerful and optimistic during a hard time.

idiom: Leave a Bad Taste in Your Mouth
choices: create an unpleasant feeling that lingers
make someone feel disappointed after high expectations
japanese: 後味が悪い、不快な印象を残す
explanation: Means an experience leaves you with a lasting feeling of disgust or discomfort.

idiom: Make a Mountain Out of a Molehill
choices: treat a small problem as if it is much bigger than it is
exaggerate how difficult something will be
literal: モグラ塚から山を作る
japanese: 針小棒大に騒ぐ、些細なことを大げさにする
explanation: Means to exaggerate the importance or seriousness of a minor problem.

choices: not something I enjoy or like
not something I am good at
japanese: 好みではない、自分には合わない
explanation: Means something is not appealing or enjoyable to you personally.

choices: making progress in the correct direction
following the right plan or approach
japanese: 正しい方向に進んでいる、順調に進んでいる
explanation: Means you are heading in the right direction and your approach is working.

choices: figure out the truth by combining obvious clues
reach the most logical conclusion from given information
japanese: 証拠を合わせて理解する、状況から察する
explanation: Means to reach an obvious conclusion by combining available pieces of information.

choices: avoid committing to either side of a disagreement
wait and see before making a judgment
japanese: どちらの立場も取らない、曖昧な態度をとる
explanation: Describes someone who refuses to take a clear position on an issue.

choices: a rough or approximate number
the expected range within which a result will fall
japanese: おおよその数字、だいたいの見積もり
explanation: Refers to a rough estimate rather than a precise number.

choices: the most important point or final conclusion
the financial result of a business decision
japanese: 要するに、最も重要な点
explanation: Refers to the most essential fact or the final result of a situation.

idiom: The Early Bird Catches the Worm
choices: those who start early have an advantage
being prepared leads to better results
literal: 早起きの鳥が虫を捕まえる
japanese: 早起きは三文の得、先に行動した人が有利
explanation: Means that people who begin something early are more likely to succeed.

choices: something very unwelcome and frustrating right now
an additional burden added to existing problems
japanese: 今一番困ること、これ以上は勘弁してほしいこと
explanation: Expresses that something is very unwanted, especially given the current situation.

choices: the most important aspect of a particular situation
the skill or quality needed to succeed
japanese: 最も重要な要素、肝心なこと
explanation: Means the most crucial factor in a given activity or situation.

choices: clear signs that something bad is going to happen
evidence that a situation is already beyond saving
japanese: 不吉な前兆、悪いことが起きる予感
explanation: Means there are obvious signs that something bad or undesirable is about to happen.

idiom: There's No Point Crying Over Spilled Milk
choices: don't waste energy regretting what cannot be undone
small mistakes aren't worth getting upset about
literal: こぼれたミルクを泣いても仕方がない
japanese: 後悔しても始まらない、覆水盆に返らず
explanation: Means there is no use being upset about something that has already happened and cannot be changed.

choices: respond quickly and cleverly without preparation
make a decision while still gathering information
japanese: 即座に対応する、臨機応変に考える
explanation: Means to think quickly and react to situations without much time to prepare.

choices: discourage or dampen enthusiasm for something
point out the flaws in someone's idea
japanese: 水を差す、熱意をそぐ
explanation: Means to discourage or reduce enthusiasm for a plan or idea.

choices: confuse or distract someone from what they were doing
surprise someone in a way that causes them to react poorly
japanese: 人を混乱させる、ペースを乱す
explanation: Means to confuse or unsettle someone so they lose their focus or composure.

choices: used to introduce a sincere or blunt opinion
used to admit something you were not going to say
japanese: 正直に言うと、本当のことを言えば
explanation: A phrase used to signal you are about to give your true or candid opinion.

idiom: To Make a Long Story Short
choices: to summarize without going into all the details
to skip the unnecessary parts of a story
literal: 長い話を短くするために
japanese: 手短に言えば、かいつまんで言うと
explanation: Said when you want to give a brief summary instead of a full explanation.

choices: used to suggest that the truth is actually stronger than stated
used to show you are being very careful with your words
japanese: 控えめに言っても、まだ言い足りないくらいだが
explanation: Used after a statement to suggest the reality is even more extreme than what was said.

choices: change your behavior and start fresh
make a decision to leave a bad situation behind
literal: 新しいページをめくる
japanese: 心を入れ替える、新たな出発をする
explanation: Means to make a fresh start and improve your behavior or way of life.

choices: done secretly and illegally, often involving money
handled privately without involving others
japanese: 裏取引で、非公式に（違法な）やりとりをする
explanation: Refers to payments or dealings that are done secretly and dishonestly.

choices: fully informed and current on a situation
capable of performing at the required level
literal: スピードまで上がっている
japanese: 最新の情報を持っている、状況を把握している
explanation: Means having all the latest information about a situation or topic.

choices: carefully balance between two difficult or opposing things
act cautiously to avoid causing offense
japanese: 微妙なバランスを保つ、二つの難しいことの間を慎重に進む
explanation: Means to balance carefully between two equally difficult or contradictory requirements.

choices: be careful about your actions or words
pay attention to potential dangers ahead
japanese: 言動に気をつけろ、慎重に行動しろ
explanation: A warning to be careful about how you behave or what you say.

idiom: We'll Cross That Bridge When We Come to It
choices: deal with a problem when it actually happens, not before
wait until the right moment to make a difficult decision
literal: その橋に来たら渡ろう
japanese: その時はその時で考えよう、問題が起きてから対処する
explanation: Means not to worry about a problem until it actually arises.

choices: how you treat others will eventually come back to you
bad behavior always leads to bad consequences in the end
literal: 回り回ってまた戻ってくる
japanese: 因果応報、まいた種は必ず戻ってくる
explanation: Means your actions, whether good or bad, will eventually return to affect you.

choices: when bad things happen, many tend to happen at once
once something starts, it is difficult to stop
literal: 雨が降るとき、どしゃ降りになる
japanese: 泣きっ面に蜂、悪いことは重なるもの
explanation: Means that problems or events tend to come all at once rather than one at a time.

choices: if there are signs of a problem, something is likely wrong
rumors always have some truth behind them
literal: 煙があるところには火がある
japanese: 火のないところに煙は立たない、うわさには根拠がある
explanation: Means that if there are signs or rumors of a problem, something real is likely behind them.

idiom: You Can't Judge a Book by Its Cover
choices: don't form an opinion based on appearances alone
don't assume you know what something is until you look closely
literal: 表紙で本を判断してはいけない
japanese: 外見で判断するな、見かけだけで決めるな
explanation: Warns against making judgments based solely on how something or someone looks.

choices: we gain wisdom through experience and mistakes
everyone makes mistakes, so it's important to keep trying
japanese: 経験から学ぶもの、生きていれば色々勉強になる
explanation: Expresses that we gain knowledge from our life experiences and mistakes.

choices: life is short, so take chances and enjoy it
make the most of every opportunity you are given
japanese: 人生は一度だけ、思い切ってやれ
explanation: Encourages taking risks or trying new things because you only have one life to live.

choices: the time allowed for something has ended
you have lost your chance to do something
literal: あなたの時間は終わった
japanese: 時間切れだ、制限時間が来た
explanation: Means that the allotted time for something has expired.

choices: a guess made with very little information
an attempt that has almost no chance of success
japanese: 当てずっぽう、根拠のない推測
explanation: Means a guess or attempt made without much information to guide it.

choices: a hopeless search that wastes time and effort
a complicated task with too many dead ends
japanese: 無駄骨を折る、徒労に終わる追いかけ
explanation: Refers to a pointless or unproductive search that leads nowhere.

choices: confused and not knowing what to do or say
unable to solve a problem despite trying hard
japanese: 途方に暮れる、どうすればいいかわからない
explanation: Describes a state of total uncertainty about what to do or say next.

choices: easily and immediately available
within your ability to do or achieve
japanese: すぐ手の届くところにある、いつでも使える
explanation: Means something is very easily accessible or available whenever you need it.

choices: please go ahead and do it
feel free to help yourself to something
japanese: どうぞ遠慮なく、どうぞ好きにしてください
explanation: Used to give someone enthusiastic permission to do something.

choices: finish something before the time runs out
work faster than expected to complete a task
japanese: 時間内に終わらせる、締め切りに間に合わせる
explanation: Means to complete something before the deadline or time limit expires.

choices: break or ignore a rule slightly to suit the situation
interpret a rule in a flexible or creative way
japanese: 規則を多少無視する、融通を利かせる
explanation: Means to slightly break or ignore a rule when it seems acceptable to do so.

choices: agree that nobody owes the other anything
decide a contest or situation is a tie
japanese: 貸し借りなしにする、引き分けにする
explanation: Means to consider a situation balanced with no debt or obligation remaining.

choices: return to the original situation after many changes
finish something by returning to how it began
literal: 完全な円を描いて戻る
japanese: 元の状態に戻る、一巡する
explanation: Means a situation has developed and returned to the same point where it started.

choices: stop doing something annoying or unacceptable
remove the unnecessary parts of something
japanese: やめろ、いい加減にしろ
explanation: An informal command to stop doing something that is bothering someone.

choices: at some point in the future
later in a process or sequence of events
japanese: 将来的に、いずれ
explanation: Refers to something that will happen or be relevant at a later point in time.

choices: set a limit on what you will accept or do
make a clear decision between two options
japanese: 一線を引く、限界を決める
explanation: Means to establish a clear boundary on what you consider unacceptable.

choices: be overlooked or missed when it shouldn't be
fail to be completed due to poor organization
japanese: 見落とされる、すり抜けてしまう
explanation: Means something or someone is missed or ignored by a system that should have handled it.

choices: control your emotions and calm down
take responsibility and deal with a situation seriously
japanese: 落ち着け、自分を取り戻せ
explanation: Means to take control of your emotions and stop overreacting.

choices: start a process or activity moving
take the first step in a long project
literal: ボールを転がし始める
japanese: 物事を始める、開始する
explanation: Means to initiate or begin a process so that progress can be made.

choices: stop being so critical or harsh toward someone
allow someone time to recover from stress
japanese: 大目に見てやる、責めるのをやめる
explanation: Means to stop being so hard on someone or to give them a chance.

choices: make extra effort to do something special for someone
do more than expected in a particular situation
japanese: わざわざ手間をかける、特別な努力をする
explanation: Means to make a special effort to do something beyond what is normally required.

choices: wait patiently while something is being resolved
stay calm even though a situation is difficult
literal: しっかりつかまっていろ
japanese: じっと待っていてください、しばらく待って
explanation: Means to hold on and wait patiently for a short time.

choices: maintain your current position and not give in
keep things from getting worse while waiting for help
japanese: 現状を維持する、立場を守る
explanation: Means to maintain a position, standard, or level without backing down.

choices: to summarize briefly and clearly
in the simplest possible terms
japanese: 要するに、ひと言で言えば
explanation: Means to say something in a very brief and clear way.

choices: in a similar manner or style
on a related or connected topic
japanese: 同じ流れで、同様に
explanation: Means continuing in the same manner or spirit as something just mentioned.

choices: continue doing what you are doing well
don't stop making progress toward your goal
japanese: その調子で続けて、頑張って
explanation: Encouragement to continue with something you are doing successfully.

choices: choose not to react to something you noticed
forgive someone for something and move on
japanese: 大目に見る、見て見ぬふりをする
explanation: Means to ignore something or decide not to respond to it.

choices: use an opportunity in the best possible way
ensure your effort produces a meaningful result
literal: それを意味のあるものにする
japanese: 無駄にしないようにする、精一杯やる
explanation: Means to make sure something is used or done in a worthwhile and effective way.

choices: accept the offer as it is or don't take it at all
decide now whether you want it or not
japanese: 受け入れるかどうかは相手次第、これ以上は譲れない
explanation: Means the offer is final and the other person must either accept it completely or refuse it.
